- **Event date columns**: one column per league event, labeled with the event's date. Each cell shows the player's contribution from that event. Tap a cell to jump into the underlying tournament's leaderboard.

### Cumulative scoring

By default, every event adds to a running total. The league format's scoring type determines exactly how a single event is converted into a number (sum, average, FedEx points, etc.).

### Eclectic composite scorecard

For an **Individual eclectic** league format, the **Total** column holds each player's composite score built from their best score on every hole across the season. Tap a player's total to open their **composite scorecard**: a front-nine and back-nine grid showing the best score recorded on each hole and, under each score, the date of the event it came from. Tap a date to jump to that event's leaderboard.

### Dropped scores (best-of-N)

The league format has an optional **Number of events to use** setting. When set to N, only each player's best N event scores count toward the leaderboard total. Worse scores are automatically **dropped**, and dropped event cells are colored red so it is obvious which scores are not counting. A footer line at the bottom of the leaderboard says "Best N of M events" so spectators understand what they are looking at.

### Minimum events to rank

The league-level **Min events to rank** setting requires a player to complete at least that many events before they can be ranked. Players below the minimum still appear, with their total in red, but their position is not displayed.

### FedEx points

If the league format is a FedEx-style points format, each event awards points by finishing place using a configurable **points distribution**. Place is taken from the **flight a player actually competed in for that event**, so points reflect how they finished within their own flight rather than the whole field. The points distribution can be edited from **Edit League Format**, and individual events can override the league default with a custom distribution (useful for "majors" worth more points). Other configuration options on FedEx formats:

- **Average points**: averages the player's earned points across the events they entered instead of summing them.
- **Points for ties**: when two players tie for a place, the points for that place and the next are either split (averaged) or fully awarded to both (full points).

The league leaderboard shows each player's cumulative or averaged FedEx total in the **Total** column; the event columns show the points earned for that event.

## Public Web Pages and Sharing

Every Squabbit group (tournament, league, or club) can be opened in a browser without installing the app, but the admin controls whether to advertise that publicly.

### Public website toggle

From **Edit Tournament** (or **Edit League** or **Edit Club**) open the **Web Browser** section and turn on **Generate HTML website**. The public website is a **Host Pro** feature (see the Host Pro and Purchases section in the settings guide). Once on, a URL is shown that anyone can open in a normal browser. The URL has the format `app.squabbitgolf.com/w/<groupType>/<groupId>`. Tap the URL row to copy it to your clipboard and open it.

Public visitors see a leaderboard-style view of the group. Members who are signed in see the full app with everything they have permission to see. Players who are not in the tournament but who hold the spectator link can follow scoring live without taking up a registration slot.

### Embedding the leaderboard on your own site

Once **Generate HTML website** is on, the Web Browser section also shows a **Leaderboard embed** row. Tap it to copy a snippet of HTML to your clipboard, then paste it into your own website where you want the live leaderboard to appear. It embeds just the live leaderboard (not the whole group page) and updates automatically as scores come in.

### Sharing menu

The invite dialog (reached from the **share** button on any group page) gives you four ways to share:

- **Tournament link** (or league/club link): a deep link that opens the tournament directly in the app, or in the browser if the app is not installed.
- **Invite code**: a six-character code. Players can type the code into **Enter invite code** on the home tab to join without a link. Tapping the code in the dialog copies it.
- **QR code**: a QR encoding the tournament link. Tap the QR button to display the code full-screen for someone to scan.
- **Spectator link**: appears only on tournaments and leagues; copies the public website URL. The first time you copy the spectator link, **Generate HTML website** is auto-enabled so the link works immediately.

### What spectators see

When someone hits the public website without being signed in, they see a stripped-down view:

- The home tab with the sponsor carousel, any text/image/map/contact/schedule widgets the admin built, and the photo carousel.
- The leaderboard tab with all formats, flights, and rounds (subject to the **Hide leaderboard results** rule and the **Show for admins** override).
- The activity tab (if **Activity tab visible for spectators** is on) with hole-by-hole notable events as they happen.

Spectators cannot enter scores, register, chat, or upload photos. They can refresh and watch live; updates push through automatically.

### Tab visibility for spectators

Tournament-level switches let admins decide what spectators see:

- **Leaderboard tab visible**
- **Schedule tab visible**
- **Activity tab visible**, with a separate **Activity tab visible for spectators** sub-switch (so members and spectators can have different views of the activity feed).

Turning a tab off hides it from both the app and the public web view.

### Photo carousel

Every group has a photo carousel on the home tab. Players can upload pictures from their phone; the latest photos rotate at the top of the home page. Tap any photo to open the full-screen carousel.

Inside the full-screen view you can:

- Swipe left and right to scroll through every photo in the group.
- Tap the menu (top right) and choose **Download photo** to save the image to your device.
- See an attribution chip in the bottom-right corner showing who uploaded the photo (their avatar and name); tap it to open their profile.

Permissions:

- Any group member can upload a photo.
- Any group member can download any photo.
- A photo can be deleted by the person who uploaded it. Group admins can delete any photo.

## PDFs and Printing (admin)

Squabbit can print several kinds of physical assets from any tournament. Access is the same for all of them: from **Edit Tournament** tap **Print tournament assets**, which opens the **Print assets** page. That page first shows a chooser listing the available asset types as tiles: Scorecards, Cart signs, Tee sheet, Hole competition signs, and (for elimination formats) Bracket. Tap a type to open its setup and options. Printing is a **Host Pro** feature (see the Host Pro and Purchases section in the settings guide).

The bottom-right button is labeled **Preview** on mobile (it generates the PDF and opens an in-app preview where you can print or share) and **Print** on web (it triggers a direct download of the PDF, and warns you up front how long it will take based on the number of pages).

### Common settings

- **Tournament round**: which round the assets are for. If the tournament has only one round it is auto-selected.
- **Tournament format**: the format to use for scoring layout (only matters for scorecards and only appears when the tournament has more than one format). The picker only lists formats that actually apply to the selected round, and if you switch rounds Squabbit re-points it to a format that covers the new round, so you never accidentally print handicap-less cards from a format that isn't enabled for that round.
- **Logo**: tap to upload a custom logo from your device. The logo appears in the header of every printed asset. Tap an existing logo to edit or remove it.
- **Sponsors**: if the tournament has sponsors with logos, a **Sponsors** section lists each one with a switch. Turn a sponsor on to print its logo in the header of this and every other printed asset (scorecards, cart signs, tee sheets, hole-competition signs). A sponsor with no logo can't be printed, so its switch is disabled. The setting is saved, so your choice sticks across assets and sessions.
- **Assets per page**: choose 1 or 2 per page. Available for scorecards, cart signs, and hole-competition signs. Not available for tee sheets (they always pack one player per row).

### Scorecards

The scorecard PDF includes for each tee time:

- A **header** with the tournament name, course, round, date, group number, starting hole, tee time, and the flight names of the players. If you uploaded a custom logo it appears on the left and the Squabbit logo plus a **QR code** appear on the right. The QR code links to the digital scorecard so a player can scan it to open scoring on their phone.
- A **scorecard table** with the hole numbers, tee distances (per tee in use, with a short tee abbreviation if multiple tees), par row, stroke-index (SI) row, and one row per player or per team.
- **Handicap dots** on each player's cells indicating how many strokes they get on that hole (filled dots for strokes given, hollow dots for strokes received in negative-handicap or plus games).
- **Hole competition highlights**: cells for closest-to-the-pin, longest drive, straightest drive, custom, and message-only competitions are tinted (blue, green, purple, orange) so players know which holes count.
- A **bottom message line** combining your custom message and a summary of hole competitions ("Long drive on Hole 5, KP on Hole 8").
- A **signature row** with two lines (player and marker).

Scorecard-specific switches:

- **Print empty scorecards**: appends N blank scorecards to the end of the PDF (for walk-ups). The default is 4 but you can change it.
- **One player per scorecard**: switches from the default 4-up format to one scorecard per player. Useful for individual-stroke-play events.
- **One team per scorecard**: same idea but for team formats. Mutually exclusive with one-player-per-scorecard.
- **Include marker row** (sub-option of the per-player/per-team modes): adds a **tear-off marker** strip at the bottom of each scorecard. The marker can sign at the bottom while the player keeps the top half.
- **Show pace of play**: adds a pace-of-play row to the scorecard showing the expected cumulative time at each hole based on the tournament's per-par-3/par-4/par-5 pace settings. Only appears if **Show pace of play indicator** is on at the tournament level.
- **Custom scorecard message**: appears under each scorecard, up to 300 characters. The message is stored on the round, so different rounds can have different messages.

### Cart signs

Cart signs are one large printable per cart that goes on the dashboard. The PDF builds one per cart per group. Each sign shows:

- Custom logo (if uploaded).
- Tournament name, starting hole, tee time.
- The names of the riders, in large type.
- The Squabbit logo and a **QR code** scoped to just that cart's two players. Scanning the QR puts only those two players into the scorecard, which speeds up score entry compared to a four-player QR.

### Tee sheet (starter sheet)

The tee sheet is a one-page (or multi-page) roster of who is playing when. Open the **Sort by** option to choose:

- **Tee time**: organized by group, in tee-off order, with a header row per group. Good for the starter to call in groups.
- **Last name A-Z**: organized alphabetically by surname. Good for a posted roster.

Tap **Tee sheet columns** to pick which player attributes to include. Available columns:

- **Hdcp** (handicap, on by default)
- **Tee** (default on if the field uses multiple tees)
- **Gender** (default on if the field is mixed)
- **Transport** (cart/walk/etc., default on if mixed)
- **WHS ID**
- **DOB**
- **Flight** (default on if the tournament has flights)
- One column per **custom user field** the tournament has defined.

For multi-course rounds, a course picker lets you generate one tee sheet per course.

### Hole competition signs

Generates a printable sign-up sheet for each distance-based hole competition (closest-to-the-pin, longest drive, straightest drive). Each sign shows the competition title (custom message if you set one, otherwise the competition type), the hole number, a distance label using the player's preferred unit (yards or meters), the prize line (if a purse is attached, the distribution summary), and a blank table where players can write their name and their distance. Spectators or marshals can score-keep right on the sheet.

Hole-competition signs include the custom logo and the Squabbit logo at the top.

### Bracket

For **Elimination** and **Team Elimination** formats, a **Bracket** asset prints the knockout bracket as a landscape PDF: the tournament name and logo, round labels, every match with its players or teams (team members listed), scores where matches have been played, BYE slots, and empty slots for matches not yet decided. The drawing follows the format's **Bracket layout** setting (left to right, or out to in with the final in the center). If the format has flights, each flight gets its own bracket page.

### Where files end up

- **Mobile**: the PDF is written to a temporary file and shown in an in-app preview. From the preview you can hit **Print** (uses the system print sheet) or **Share** to send it via AirDrop/email/etc.
- **Web**: the PDF downloads directly to your browser. Web builds can take a while if the field is large, so the dialog tells you approximately how long the export will take before it starts.

### Multi-tee handling on scorecards

When a group has players on different tees, Squabbit prints the scorecard table with one tee distance/par row per tee in use, capped at two tees per scorecard. Each player row has a small tee abbreviation in parentheses next to their name (for example "Smith (Bl)" / "Jones (Wh)") so you can tell who is playing from which tee box. The handicap stroke dots are calculated from each player's actual tee.

### Quota games

If the round's game template uses **Quota** scoring, the per-hole dot pattern is replaced with empty cells. Quotas are about point totals rather than stroke-by-stroke handicapping, so the scorecard PDF leaves the per-hole cells blank so players can write their own quota math.

### Ghost scorecards

If a tournament has ghost players (linked-handicap or sub-for placeholder users), their scorecards print like any other player's. The ghost's linked handicap is shown in the name cell. Ghosts get the standard QR code that opens their scorecard in the app, so they can be score-keyed by whomever is subbing for them.

### Edit Hole Markers (Admin Map View)

The scorecard has a map view that shows each hole's tee, fairway, pin, and hazard markers. Admins can edit those markers and save them back to the course definition so they feed both the on-course distance display and the markings on printed cart signs.

To edit markers:

1. From the scorecard, switch to **Map view**.
2. Open the scorecard overflow menu (three dots, top right). With map view active, an **Edit hole markers** option appears.
3. Drag any tee, fairway, pin, or hazard marker on the map to its correct location. Save when you are done.

The updated markers persist on the course record, so every future round on that course benefits. Use this when you notice the printed cart-sign distances are off or the on-course tracker is pinning the wrong spot.

### Riding partners reminder

For cart signs the print page surfaces a small **Riding partners** info tile. Tapping it explains that pairing inside a group (who rides with whom) is decided by the order players appear inside the scorecard. To rearrange riding partners, reorder the players inside the group from the **Tee times** page; then re-generate cart signs.

## Sponsors (admin)

Sponsors are managed from the **Sponsors** page (reached from the group's settings). Sponsors are a **Host Pro** feature (see the Host Pro and Purchases section in the settings guide). Every sponsor has a name, a logo, an optional website URL, and three placement options:

- **Show on home**: the sponsor appears in the home-tab carousel.
- **Show on leaderboard**: the sponsor appears in the leaderboard-tab carousel.
- **Sponsored holes**: a per-round, per-hole map. Sponsors assigned to a hole get their logo highlighted on that hole in the printed scorecard.

The carousel rotates through every visible sponsor automatically for the first loop and then stops. Tap any logo to open the sponsor's website. If no URL was set, the tap does nothing.

### Adding a sponsor

From the **Sponsors** page tap the **+** floating action button. You will be asked whether to:

- **Create new sponsor**: opens the **Edit sponsor** page where you enter name, logo (uploaded from your photo library), website URL, and the three placement switches. For tournaments, you can additionally tap **Sponsored holes** to attach the sponsor to specific holes on specific rounds.
- **Copy from another tournament**: opens the copy flow, where you pick another tournament or league you admin, multi-select which of its sponsors to bring over, and decide whether to copy hole assignments too. When copying holes, Squabbit maps source-tournament round indexes to destination-tournament round indexes (round 1 to round 1, round 2 to round 2, etc.); source rounds with no destination counterpart are dropped.

### Editing and reordering

Tap any sponsor in the list to open an action sheet with **Edit sponsor** and **Remove sponsor**. Edit opens the same page used for creation. Remove asks for confirmation, then deletes the sponsor.

When you have more than one sponsor, every row gets a drag handle on the right. Long-press the handle and drag to reorder. The order in the list is the order sponsors appear in the carousel.

### Where sponsors show up

- **Home carousel**: at the top of the group's Home tab, just above the home widgets. Built from sponsors with **Show on home** on.
- **Leaderboard carousel**: at the top of the Leaderboard tab. Built from sponsors with **Show on leaderboard** on. A preview of the leaderboard carousel appears at the top of the **Sponsors** page itself so you can verify it.
- **Printed scorecards**: hole cells get a subtle highlight on holes that have a sponsor assigned, and the sponsor names can appear in the bottom message strip of the scorecard.
- **Printed asset headers**: any sponsor you switch on in the **Sponsors** section of the Print assets page has its logo printed in the header of every asset (scorecards, cart signs, tee sheets, hole-competition signs). See Logos on PDFs below.

The carousel is always visible on the home and leaderboard tabs (when there are sponsors); there is no way for a player to dismiss it.

### Logos on PDFs

Sponsor logos can be printed on physical assets. On the **Print assets** page, the **Sponsors** section lists every sponsor that has a logo, each with a switch. Turn a sponsor on and its logo prints in the header of every printed asset (scorecards, cart signs, tee sheets, and hole-competition signs), alongside your uploaded **custom logo** (typically the host club or the tournament's own logo) and the Squabbit logo. The toggle is saved per sponsor, so it applies across all asset types. A sponsor with no logo uploaded can't be printed, so its switch is greyed out until you add one.

### Removing a sponsor's hole assignment

To unassign a sponsor from a hole without deleting the sponsor entirely, open the sponsor with **Edit sponsor**, tap **Sponsored holes**, and tap the hole again to toggle it off. The sponsor stays in the carousel but loses its scorecard highlight on that hole.

## Group Home Page Customization (admin)

Every group (tournament, league, or club) has a **Home tab** that the admin can build out of widgets. The widget system lets you mix free-form content with built-in views like the schedule and the venue map.

### Edit mode

If you are an admin, an **Edit / Preview** toggle appears at the top right of the Home tab. Tap it to switch into edit mode. While editing:

- Each widget is wrapped in a card with a header showing its type and icon.
- A drag handle on the right lets you long-press and reorder widgets.
- A red trash icon deletes the widget. For row widgets that contain child widgets, you are asked whether to **Keep widgets** (unpacks the children back into the main list) or **Delete all** (removes the row and everything inside).
- A blue **Add widget** button at the top lets you append a new widget. Tapping it opens a bottom sheet listing every available widget type.

Switch back to **Preview** to see how players will see the page. Changes auto-save with a short debounce so you do not need to hit a save button.

### Widget types

- **Text**: rich text block with formatting. Use for welcome messages, schedules, rules, prize information, etc.
- **Image**: a single image you upload. Tap in edit mode to swap the image.
- **Schedule** (tournaments only): renders the tournament's rounds, dates, tee-time list, and optional weather chips. There is no per-tournament configuration; the schedule is generated from the rounds you already created. If you have not entered tee times, the widget falls back to showing just the round dates.
- **Map**: an interactive map pinned to the group's location. Tapping it opens the address in the device's mapping app.
- **Contact**: shows the contact info you entered for the group (organizer name, phone, email) as a tappable list.
- **Donation** (tournaments only): a fundraising card for one charity with a progress bar and a **Donate** button. Adding a charity in the Money tab automatically places one of these on the home tab; in edit mode you can point it at a different charity.
- **Row**: a layout container that holds two child widgets side by side. The row supports three column ratios:
  - **Half / half** (50/50)
  - **One-third / two-thirds**
  - **Two-thirds / one-third**

  On narrow screens (under 550 pixels wide, i.e. most phones in portrait), row widgets automatically stack their children vertically so the page stays readable.

### Defaults

A brand-new group starts with a sensible default layout:

- Tournaments default to **Schedule** at the top, followed by a row containing the **Map** on one side and **Contact** on the other.
- Leagues and clubs default to just the map-and-contact row.

The defaults render until the admin makes their first edit; from that point your saved layout is what is shown.

### Limits

You can have up to **30 widgets per home tab**. Once you reach the limit the **Add widget** button is disabled and labeled "Widget limit reached (30)".

### Where sponsors fit in

The **sponsor carousel** is rendered automatically at the very top of the home tab, above your widgets. There is no widget for sponsors; manage them through the **Sponsors** page instead. The widgets you add live below the carousel.

### Reordering with drag handles

In edit mode, every widget card has a grey **drag handle** icon next to the trash. Long-press the handle and drag up or down to move the widget. The list updates instantly and the new order auto-saves. If you accidentally tap the handle instead of long-pressing, a small hint snackbar reminds you that you need to hold and drag.

### Image widget

The image widget renders a single full-width image. In edit mode, tap the image to pick a new one from your photo library or take a new picture. The image is uploaded to Squabbit's storage and a URL is saved into the widget. Replacing an image automatically deletes the old one, so storage does not accumulate stale uploads.

### Text widget

The text widget supports a basic rich-text style: bolding, italics, headings, bulleted lists, and inline links. The text shows up identically on the public website and inside the app. There is no character limit, but very long text widgets are scrollable inside their cards.

### Map widget

The map widget shows a small interactive map pinned to the group's saved location. Edit-mode users can adjust the pinned location from the widget's edit menu. Players tap the map to open it full-screen, where another tap opens directions in their device's default mapping app (Apple Maps or Google Maps).

### Schedule widget

The schedule widget pulls from the tournament's rounds, courses, dates, and tee times. Each round becomes a section with the round number, date, its **start time** (when a specific time of day is set on the round), course name, and an optional **weather chip** showing the forecast for the round's date and location (when available). Tee times are listed inside each round section. When the tournament has a registration open/close window set, a **registration window** row appears above the rounds; anyone viewing can tap it to add the window to their own calendar. There is nothing to configure on the widget; everything comes from the tournament itself, so you edit the schedule by editing rounds and the registration window, not by editing the widget.

### Contact widget

The contact widget renders the organizer's contact info you entered in **General group settings**. It typically shows a name, email, and phone number, each as a tappable row that opens the relevant app (mail, dialer). If you have not entered contact info, the widget shows an empty state prompting the admin to add it.

### Donation widget

The donation widget shows one charity's name, description, the amount raised so far, and (if the charity has a fundraising goal) a progress bar and percentage of goal. A list of recent donors and their amounts appears below, with anonymous donors shown as "Anonymous donor." Anyone viewing the group taps the **Donate** button to open the donate sheet and pay through Stripe. Squabbit adds this widget automatically when you create a charity; you can reassign it to another charity from its edit menu. See the Charity Donations section in the setup guide for how to set up and manage charities.

### Row widget for two-column layouts

The row widget exists so you can put two widgets next to each other on tablets and desktops while still falling back to a stacked layout on phones. To set it up, add a row, then drop other widgets into its left and right slots. Each slot can hold any widget type except another row (no nested rows). Common layouts:

- A text widget on the left explaining the tournament, plus an image on the right.
- A map on the left and contact info on the right (this is the default for new groups).
- A text widget on the left and a schedule on the right.

In edit mode, tapping a row's layout selector lets you switch between the three ratios. When the screen is too narrow for side-by-side display, the row's children stack vertically with the left widget on top.

---

# Communication and Feed

## Communication and Social Features

Squabbit connects golfers and organizers through chat, polls, notifications, a personalized feed, and a social follow system. This section explains how each of these features works so you can get the most out of the app.

## Chat

### Every Group Has Its Own Chat Room

Every tournament, league, and club you belong to has a dedicated chat room. You reach it by tapping the **Chat** tab inside any group's page. The chat tab icon shows a small red dot whenever there are unread messages waiting for you in that room, so you never miss activity while browsing other parts of the group.

Chat rooms are shared among all members of the group. Everyone who has been added to the tournament, league, or club can read and post unless the organizer has turned off member messaging (in which case only admins can send messages, but everyone can still read).

### Sending Text Messages

At the bottom of any chat screen you will find the compose bar. Type your message in the text field and tap the arrow/send button to post it. Messages appear in reverse-chronological order. Newest at the bottom. The stream scrolls down automatically when a new message arrives while you are viewing the chat.

URLs in messages are automatically detected and displayed as tappable links. Tapping a link opens it in your device's default browser.

### Sending Photos

Tap the **+** (attachment) button in the compose bar and choose **Image** from the menu. On mobile you can pick a photo from your camera roll or take a new photo with the camera. On the web you can upload from your file system. The photo uploads with a loading indicator, then appears in the conversation as a full-width image. Tapping any photo in chat opens it in a full-screen viewer.

You can also send **GIFs and images straight from your keyboard**: if your phone's keyboard has a GIF, sticker, or image picker (like Gboard's GIF button), choosing one inserts it into the compose bar and it is sent as an image message. This works in every chat composer, group chats and direct chats alike.

### Replying to a Message

Long-press any text or photo message to bring up the message options menu. Tap **Reply** to start a threaded reply. A compact preview of the message you are replying to appears above the compose bar to remind you of the context. Your reply will then show a condensed grey quote bubble above your own bubble so anyone reading can see what you were responding to. Tap the X next to the preview to cancel the reply before sending.

### Reactions

Long-press any message to open the emoji picker. A wide selection of emoji is shown, from thumbs up, hearts, and golf flags to fire, party poppers, and more. Tapping an emoji attaches it as your reaction to that message. Each person can have only one active reaction per message. Tapping the emoji again when it is already your reaction removes it. Tapping a different emoji replaces your existing reaction. Emoji reactions are shown as small text icons below the message bubble; tapping the emoji display opens a list showing exactly who reacted and with which emoji.

### Editing and Deleting Messages

**Editing:** Long-press one of your own text messages within 15 minutes of sending it to see the **Edit Message** option. A dialog opens pre-filled with your original text. Edit it and confirm to save. An "edited" label appears below the bubble afterward so other participants know the message was revised. Polls have their own editing flow (see Polls below).

**Deleting:** Long-press any message you sent to see the **Delete Message** option (shown in red). Group admins can delete any message from any member. You will be asked to confirm before the message is removed.

**Copying text:** The options menu also includes **Copy Text** to put the message content onto your clipboard.

### Read Receipts

After you send a message, small avatar icons appear beneath your bubble showing the people who have already viewed the conversation up to that point. This gives you instant feedback on who has seen your message without needing to ask.

### System Messages

The chat stream automatically posts system notifications for notable group events. When a player registers or unregisters, when a round finishes, when the leaderboard changes, when a new event is added to a league, and when a player scores a hole-in-one or eagle. These messages are centered in the stream and displayed without a sender bubble or chat-style background. They are informational and cannot be replied to or reacted to.

## Admin Activity Feed and Audit Log

Every tournament, league, and club keeps an **admin-only feed** that records changes made to the group, so organizers can see who did what and when. It doubles as a **private admin chat** room.

### Finding it

In the group's activity tab, admins see an **Admin** chip alongside the Chat (and Photos) chip. Tap it to switch the feed to the admin view. Only admins can see this feed; it isn't visible to players or spectators. Admin-room push notifications deep-link straight to it.

### What it logs

The feed keeps a running log of:

- **Money**: fee and expense changes, purse changes, and every payment event (succeeded, failed, refunded, claimed, confirmed, requested, deleted) including manual and peer-to-peer payments.
- **Roster**: registrations and unregistrations, admins added or removed, ownership transfers, and league-event declines.
- **Scoring**: score edits and handicap overrides.
- **Setup**: rounds added or deleted, and date, course, tee, tee-time, and settings changes.
- **Lifecycle**: group creation.

Each entry is written in plain language naming the admin who made the change. You can also post regular messages here to talk privately with the other admins.

### Filtering

A row of **category filter chips** (All, Money, Roster, Scoring, Setup, Lifecycle, Chat) sits above the admin feed. Tap a category to show only those entries; tap **All** to clear the filter. An info card at the top of the history explains what the feed is.

## Direct Chats and the Inbox

Beyond group chat rooms, Squabbit supports private 1:1 conversations and small group conversations with any combination of users.

### Opening the Inbox

Tap the **inbox icon** in the top-right corner of the home feed to open the Inbox page. The Inbox has two tabs: **Chats** and **Notifications**. The inbox icon on the home feed shows a red dot whenever you have either unread chat messages or notifications you have not yet seen. The two tabs inside the Inbox each have their own red dot indicator as well, so you can immediately see which area needs attention.

### Starting a New Direct or Group Chat

Inside the Chats tab, tap the floating action button (speech bubble with a plus icon) in the bottom-right corner. Two options expand:

- **Chat**: Searches for a single user to start a private 1:1 conversation.
- **Group Chat**: Lets you pick multiple users at once to create a group conversation thread.

When you start a 1:1 chat, Squabbit checks whether a direct conversation already exists between you and that person. If it does, it opens the existing room rather than creating a duplicate.

### Chat List and Sorting

The Chats tab shows all your conversations in order of the most recent message, newest at the top. Each row displays:
- The avatars of the other participants.
- The other participants' names (up to the first few).
- A preview of the last message sent.
- The timestamp of the last message, shown in your group's primary color when the conversation has unread messages.
- A small red circle on the right when there are unread messages in that thread.

Tap any conversation row to open it. When you come back to the Chats list after reading a conversation, its unread indicator clears.

### Unread Indicators at Every Level

Squabbit tracks unread state at multiple levels so you always know where to look:

- **Home feed Inbox icon**: A red dot means you have unread messages in at least one chat room, or unseen notifications.
- **Chats tab label**: A red dot means at least one direct conversation has unread messages.
- **Notifications tab label**: A red dot means there are notifications you have not viewed.
- **Group chat tab icon**: Each tournament, league, club, or scorecard that has a chat tab shows its own red dot when messages in that group's chat room have not been seen by you.
- **Read receipts inside a conversation**: Small avatars below your sent messages show which specific people have read them.

Opening a chat or the Notifications tab clears the corresponding indicator.

## Polls

Polls can be created inside any chat room, group chats and direct chats alike, so you can gather opinions from players without leaving Squabbit.

### Creating a Poll

Tap the **+** attachment button in the compose bar and choose **Poll**. The poll editor opens as a new screen. To build your poll:

1. Tap the **Question** row to type your question.
2. Tap the floating **+** button to add each answer option. A minimum of two answers is required before you can send.
3. Tap any existing answer to edit its text or delete it.
4. Drag the handle icon next to an answer to reorder the list.
5. Configure the voting behavior:
   - **Allow changing vote**: When on, participants can update their vote after casting it. When off, votes are permanent once submitted.
   - **Allow multi-voting**: Only available when vote-changing is enabled. When on, a single participant can select more than one answer. When off, each person can pick only one answer.
6. Tap the **Send** icon (paper plane) in the app bar to confirm and post the poll to the chat room.

If you tap back without sending, you will be asked whether you want to discard your changes.

### Voting on a Poll

Tap any answer bar in the poll to cast your vote. A confirmation dialog appears listing all answers as radio buttons, letting you select your final choice before committing. Once you confirm, your vote is recorded immediately and the progress bars update for everyone.

**Changing your vote:** If the poll allows it, tapping an answer you already voted for shows options to change your vote or remove it entirely. If the poll does not allow vote changes, a message tells you your vote is locked.

**Multi-vote polls:** When multi-voting is enabled, selecting a new answer does not remove your existing votes. Each answer you tap adds to your set of selected options.

### Viewing Results

The results are visible to everyone at all times. You do not need to vote to see the current distribution. Each answer bar fills proportionally with the percentage of total votes it has received, and the vote count is shown on the right. Small avatar icons beneath each bar show which specific users voted for that option. Tapping the answer bar opens the vote confirmation dialog even after you have already voted, so you can change or review your vote.

### Editing and Deleting a Poll

The person who created the poll can edit it at any time. Long-press the poll message bubble and choose **Edit** to reopen the poll editor pre-filled with the current question, answers, and settings. Edits are saved automatically when you navigate back. You can also delete individual answers from inside the editor, or delete the entire poll message the same way you delete any other message (long-press, then Delete Message).

## Notifications

### Push Notifications

Squabbit sends push notifications to your device to keep you informed about activity across your tournaments, leagues, and clubs, as well as social events like new followers and direct messages.

### Notification Categories

All notifications fall into named categories, each of which can be independently turned on or off.

**Tournament notifications:**
- **First place changed in {format}**: Someone moves into or out of first place. This is per-format: every scoring format in the tournament has its own toggle, so admins can enable first-place-change pushes for the headline Stroke Play format while keeping them off for noisier formats like Skins. For Scramble, Ambrose, Alternate Shot, and Chapman (formats where one round score is shared by all team members), birdie notifications default to **off** when the format is added — admins are sometimes surprised by this but it prevents the same birdie from pinging the field once per teammate. Flip the toggle on per format if you want them back.
- **Special hole score**: A hole-in-one, eagle, or albatross.
- **Hole-out**: A player finishes a hole.
- **Hole outs**: A separate toggle for any holed shot from off the green — a chip-in, a pitch-in, anything that finishes a hole with a non-putting stroke. Distinct from the hole-in-one notification. Admins toggle this independently per tournament. When on, the activity feed posts "{Player} holed out on hole {N}" the moment a zero-putts hole score is saved.
- **Round finished**: A participant completes their round.
- **Match completed**: A matchplay match reaches a result.
- **New registration**: A player joins the tournament.
- **Chat message**: A new message in the tournament chat.
- **Invite**: You have been invited to join a tournament.

**League notifications:**
- **Leaderboard first-place change.**
- **New registration.**
- **Chat message.**
- **New event**: An organizer adds a new event to the league schedule.
- **Invite.**

**Club notifications:**
- **New tournament**: The club creates a new tournament.
- **New league**: The club creates a new league.
- **Chat message.**
- **Invite.**

**Social notifications:**
- **Round started**: Someone you follow begins a new round.
- **Special hole score**: Someone you follow scores a hole-in-one or eagle.
- **Round finished**: Someone you follow completes a round.
- **Chat message**: A message on a public social feed you follow.

**Scorecard notifications:**
- **Scorecard message**: A new message on a shared scorecard you are part of.

**Direct notifications:**
- **Direct chat message**: A message sent to you in a 1:1 or group direct chat.
- **Follow request**: Someone asks to follow you. The card lets you Approve or Decline (and, after approving, Follow back).
- **Follow approved**: Someone approved your follow request, so you can now see their rounds, stats, and activity.

**Payment notifications:**

When money is involved, Squabbit sends interactive payment cards to your **Notifications tab**:

- **Payment claimed**: Someone tells you they've paid (an entry fee, a settlement, or a side-game bet). The card lets you **Confirm** you received it (which clears their balance) or **Reject** it.
- **Payment requested**: Someone asks you to pay them. The card lets you **Pay** (opens the how-to-pay flow) or **Decline**.

Once a payment reaches its final state, the card shows a green terminal pill (e.g. "Payment confirmed"). These cards work for both group balances (entry fees, payouts) and scorecard side-game settlements; the side-game versions name the round instead of a group.

**Auction notifications:**

When a tournament runs a fundraiser auction, bidders get interactive cards in their **Notifications tab** (and matching emails):

- **Outbid**: someone topped your bid on an open item. The card names the item and the new minimum bid, with a **Place bid** button to jump back in.
- **Won**: your bid won an item when the auction closed. The card names the item and your winning amount, with a **View** button that opens the item (where you can pay). See the Fundraiser Auctions section in the setup guide.

**Waitlist notifications:**

When you're on a group's waitlist and a spot opens up, you get one of two interactive cards in your **Notifications tab**, depending on whether the organizer enabled auto-promote:

- **Spot available** (auto-promote off): "A spot opened up in [group]! You're next on the waitlist. Register now to claim it." Tap **View** to open the group and register before someone else does.
- **Promoted from waitlist** (auto-promote on): "A spot opened up in [group] and you've been moved off the waitlist. Complete your registration to lock in your spot." Tap **View** to finish registering (including any required fees).

### Managing Preferences Globally

Open the Inbox (inbox icon on the home feed), tap the three-dot overflow menu in the top-right corner, and choose **Notifications**. You will see all categories listed and grouped by section. Each category has a toggle switch. Turning a category off globally means you will not receive that type of notification from any group.

For tournament, league, and club chat categories (and a few others), you can also configure **per-group exceptions**. For example, muting tournament chat notifications for one specific tournament while keeping them on for all others. When exceptions are active for a category, a count badge appears next to the switch. Tap the row to open the exceptions detail page, which lists which specific groups have been muted. Tap the X next to any group name to remove the exception and restore the global setting for that group.

### Managing Preferences Per Group

From within any tournament, league, or club, tap the **bell icon** in the app bar to open that group's notification settings page. This shows only the notification categories that apply to that group type. Each toggle affects only that specific group. Your global settings for other groups are untouched. If a category is globally muted, its row appears disabled with an explanatory note; you can only re-enable it from the global Notifications page.

### The Inbox Badge

The red dot on the Inbox icon on the home feed reflects two independent signals: unread chat messages and unseen notifications. Opening the Inbox and viewing the **Notifications tab** stamps a "last seen" time that clears the notifications badge. Opening a specific chat room clears that conversation's unread badge.

### Group Invite Notifications

When someone adds you to a tournament, league, or club, a notification card appears in your **Notifications tab** showing who added you, the group name, and when it happened. From the card you can:

- **View**: Opens the group so you can review it before deciding.
- **Decline**: Removes you from the group. A confirmation dialog lets you optionally check a box to prevent that person from re-adding you in the future, and a separate option to block them entirely.
- **Undo Decline**: If you change your mind after declining, this button reverses the action: you are re-added to the group and any block placed on the inviter is removed.

## Home Feed

The home feed is the first screen you see when you open Squabbit. It is a personalized, scrollable stream of content curated to match your activity and connections.

### Feed Sections

Your feed can include any combination of the following card types, ordered by relevance and recency:

**Recent Groups**: A horizontally scrollable row of the tournaments, leagues, and clubs you have visited most recently. Tapping any card brings you straight back into that group. Each card's overflow menu lets you share the group or remove it from your recents list.

**Public Groups Nearby**: A horizontally scrollable row of publicly listed tournaments, leagues, and clubs near your current location (requires location permission). Use each card's overflow menu to mark a group as Not Interested (hides it from this section) or to report inappropriate content. If no nearby groups are available, a prompt to search for or create a public group appears in their place.

**Followed Players' Rounds**: When someone you follow starts a new scorecard, a card appears in your feed showing who is playing and on which course. If two people you follow are on the same card, both names appear in the title. Three or more shows a summary like "Alice, Bob, and 2 others started a round." Tap the card to open the live scorecard.

**Squabbit Showcase**: A horizontally scrollable carousel of featured content published by the Squabbit team. Each card displays a title and a full-bleed background image. Tapping a card may open a single link directly, or if the card has links to multiple platforms it shows a choice dialog listing the available destinations with their icons: YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, Twitter/X, a Blog, or a generic link. After you have seen a particular showcase card five times or tapped on it once, it automatically shifts to a lower position in your feed so it does not dominate the experience.

**Getting Started**: Shown to new users in place of personalized content while your feed is being populated. Contains prompts to join or create a group and links to get help.

**Ads**: Sponsored content placed at a fixed position in the feed. There is a daily cap on how many times you see each individual ad. You can dismiss an ad using its card overflow menu.

### Refreshing the Feed

The home feed is **live**: new activity (such as a player you follow starting a round) appears at the top automatically, without you having to refresh, even if the item was created while the app was in the background. You can still pull down anywhere on the feed list to force a reload at any time.

## Following and Followers

The follow system lets you keep up with the golfers you play with most. What following involves depends on the other player's **Profile visibility** setting (in their Profile settings):

- **Public** (the default): anyone can see their rounds, stats, and activity, and follow them instantly with no approval step.
- **Private**: only approved followers can see their rounds, stats, and activity, and every follow must be approved by the owner.

### Following Someone

Open any player's profile by tapping their name or avatar anywhere in the app, in a leaderboard, in a chat, on a scorecard, or by searching. If you don't follow them yet, their profile shows a **Follow** button. On a **public** profile, tapping it follows them immediately. On a **private** profile, tapping it sends a follow **request** and the button changes to **Requested**; tap **Requested** to cancel a request that hasn't been answered. Once you follow someone, the button reads **Following**; tapping it gives you the option to stop following.

Once you follow someone (instantly for public profiles, after approval for private ones):
- You can see their rounds, stats, and activity (a non-follower of a private profile sees a "This profile is private" wall instead).
- Their new rounds appear in your home feed.
- You appear in their Followers count.

### Approving Follow Requests

You only receive follow requests when your own profile visibility is set to **Private**; public profiles gain followers instantly. When someone requests to follow you, you get a **follow-request notification** in your Notifications tab and a badge on your profile. The card lets you **Approve** or **Decline**. After you approve, the card offers a **Follow back** button. You can also handle requests from a **Requests** tab on your own Connections page (next to the Followers tab), which shows a count of how many are pending. The approver and the requester are notified when a request is approved.

### Viewing a Player's Connections

On any profile page, the follower count and following count are displayed below the player's name. Tap either count to open the **Connections** page, which has two tabs: **Following** and **Followers**. Each tab paginates the full list and includes a search bar so you can quickly find a specific name. The two tabs load and search independently.

On your own Connections page, the Following tab shows a **Find people to follow** button when you are not following anyone yet or at the bottom of the full list. Tapping it opens the full user search screen where you can browse or search for players.

### Blocking

If another user sends unwanted messages on your personal chat feed, you can block them. Inside a direct message, long-press the sender's avatar to get a block option. Blocked users can no longer send messages to you in your personal chat. You can review and manage your full blocked users list: open the Inbox, tap the three-dot overflow menu, and choose **Blocked Users**.

## Photos

### Uploading Photos to a Group

Tournaments, leagues, and clubs all support a shared photo gallery that lives alongside the chat. To add a photo:

1. Inside the group's activity area, look for the **Photos** tab if the activity view supports it, then tap the **Add Photo** tile (camera icon with a + symbol).
2. Alternatively, tap the **+** button in the compose bar and choose **Image**. If you are in a group that has a media collection, the photo is added to both the chat stream and the gallery.
3. Choose a photo from your camera roll or take a new one with the camera. On the web, you can upload from your file system.

When a photo is added to the gallery, a system message also appears in the group's chat stream announcing that a photo was added, so everyone is notified.

**Who can upload:** Organizers control whether all members can add photos or only admins can. If the upload button is not visible, the organizer has restricted uploads for that group. Admins always retain the ability to upload regardless of this setting.

### Viewing Photos Full Screen

Tap any photo thumbnail to open it in the full-screen carousel. Swipe left or right to browse through all photos in the collection. The name and avatar of the person who uploaded the current photo appears in the bottom-right corner. Tap their name to visit their profile.

### Downloading and Deleting Photos

In the full-screen view, tap the three-dot menu in the top-right corner to access:

- **Download Photo**: Saves the current photo to your device's camera roll.
- **Download all photos**: Saves every photo in the group to your device at once (shown only when there is more than one photo). You're asked to confirm the number of photos, then they download to your Downloads folder.
- **Delete Photo**: Permanently removes the photo from the group. This option is only available if you are the person who uploaded the photo or you are a group admin.

### Photo Library Permission

On iOS and Android, Squabbit needs permission to access your photo library before you can select photos from your camera roll. If you have not previously granted this permission, a dialog will appear explaining how to enable it in your device's system settings.

## Social Links and the Squabbit Showcase

The Squabbit Showcase carousel in your home feed features curated content from the Squabbit team. Each showcase card can link to one or more of the following social platforms and destinations:

- **YouTube**: Video content
- **Instagram**: Photo and short video content
- **Facebook**: Page or event
- **TikTok**: Short video content
- **Twitter / X**: Posts and threads
- **Blog**: Written articles
- **Other**: Any other web link

When a showcase card links to only one destination, tapping it opens that link directly in your browser. When a card links to more than one platform, tapping it opens a choice dialog listing all available destinations with their platform icon so you can choose where to go. All links open in your device's default browser or the relevant native app if available.

---

# Settings, Errors, and FAQ

## Account, Profile, and App-Wide Settings

### Creating an Account

Squabbit offers three ways to create an account. **Google sign-in** and **Apple sign-in** (iOS only) are the fastest options. Tap the corresponding button on the sign-in screen and authenticate through your device. For **email and password**, tap "Sign Up," enter your first name, last name, email address, and a password, then accept the Terms of Service.

When you sign in for the first time through Google or Apple, Squabbit will check whether you have already been added to any tournaments, leagues, or clubs under your name. If it finds a match, it will ask "Is this you?" so your scoring history and group memberships connect automatically to your new account. You can tap "Yes, this is me" to link, or "I don't recognize this" to create a brand new profile instead.

After signing up with email, a **verification email** is sent to your address. A banner appears at the top of the home screen reminding you to verify. Tap **Verify** on the banner to open a dialog with a resend option, or tap "I've verified" once you have confirmed the link. Verification is optional for continued use but helps secure your account.

### Editing Your Profile

To edit your profile, tap your avatar on the Home tab to open your profile page, then tap the three-dot menu and choose **Profile settings**. From there you can update:

- **Name**: Tap the Name row and enter a new first and last name.
- **Profile photo and background photo**: Tap your avatar or the banner image at the top of the profile page. You can view, change, or remove the photo.
- **Home course**: Tap the Home course row to search for and select any course in Squabbit's database.
- **Handicap index**: See the Handicap section below.
- **Gender**: Used for tee assignment suggestions.
- **Date of birth**: Used for age-category calculations in some formats.
- **Bag**: Tap the Bag row to track the clubs you carry.
- **Distance preference**: Toggle between yards and meters for yardage display.
- **Temperature unit**: Toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius for weather display.
- **Wind unit**: Toggle between mph and km/h for wind speed (used on the plays-like club recommendation panel). When left unset, the unit is inferred from your country.
- **Language**: Override the app language independently of your device setting.
- **Payment methods**: Save how other people can pay you (Venmo, Zelle, PayPal, Interac, PayID, or a custom "Other" provider) with a username, phone, payment link, and/or QR code. When you win a purse or owe money in a group that settles up player-to-player, others use these handles to pay you. See the Payments section for details.
- **Profile visibility**: Choose whether your profile is **Public** or **Private**. See the next section.

### Profile Visibility (Public or Private)

In Profile settings, the **Profile visibility** setting controls who can see your golf content:

- **Public** (the default): Anyone can see your rounds, stats, and activity, and follow you instantly.
- **Private**: Only your followers can see your rounds, stats, and activity, and you approve who follows you.

When your profile is private, other players see a "This profile is private" wall with a prompt to follow you; their follow taps become **requests** that you approve or decline from your Notifications tab or the Requests tab on your Connections page. Switching to Private does not remove existing followers.

### Handicap Index Setup

In Profile settings, tap **Handicap index** to configure how your handicap is maintained. You have three source options:

- **WHS (World Handicap System)**: Links to an external handicap authority such as GHIN (North America), Golf Canada, Golf Spain, Golf South Africa, or Golf Chile. Enter your country or region and your official account number. Once linked, your handicap is fetched automatically each time you open the app or are added to a tournament. You can also tap **Refresh** at any time to pull the latest value.
- **Squabbit Index**: Squabbit calculates your handicap automatically from rounds you enter in the app. It updates after every completed round that is marked as counting toward handicap.
- **Manual**: You enter a fixed number yourself. It does not change unless you edit it again.

To link a WHS account, choose "WHS" as the source, then select your country or region and enter your account number (for example, a 7-digit GHIN number for US players). If the lookup succeeds, you will see a confirmation and your handicap will update. If it fails, see the WHS error section below.

Countries where automatic WHS lookup is currently supported include the United States, Canada, Mexico, Chile, Spain, South Africa, China, India, and the UAE. Australia, Ireland, the United Kingdom, and New Zealand do not have publicly accessible APIs available, so automatic lookup is not supported for those regions.

### Followers and Following

On any user's profile, tap the **Following** or **Followers** count to open the Connections page. From the Following tab you can search for people you follow by name. On your own profile there is also a **Find people to follow** button.

Whether following needs approval depends on the other player's **Profile visibility** setting. To follow someone, open their profile and tap **Follow**. On a **public** profile (the default) you follow them instantly. On a **private** profile this sends them a request and the button changes to **Requested**; once they approve it, their rounds, stats, and activity become visible to you and you appear in their followers. Tap **Requested** again to cancel a request that hasn't been answered yet. To stop following someone you already follow, tap **Following** on their profile and confirm.

A **private** profile walls its content off: a non-follower sees a "This profile is private" wall instead of the owner's rounds, stats, and activity. Public profiles are visible to everyone. You always see your own profile, and you can approve or decline incoming requests from the **Requests** tab (see the Communications section).

### Notifications

Tap **Notifications** in Profile settings to manage which push notifications you receive. This is covered in depth in the Communications section of this knowledge base.

### Newsletter

A toggle labeled **Subscribe to newsletter** in Profile settings controls whether you receive Squabbit update emails. You can turn it on or off at any time.

### Language

Squabbit supports eleven languages. In Profile settings tap **Language** to choose one, or leave it set to **Device default** so the app follows your operating system's language. The supported languages are English, German (Deutsch), Spanish (Español), French (Français), Italian (Italiano), Japanese (日本語), Korean (한국어), Norwegian Bokmål (Norsk bokmål), Dutch (Nederlands), Portuguese (Português), and Swedish (Svenska). The language change takes effect immediately without restarting the app.

### Changing Your Email Address

If your account uses email/password sign-in, a **Change email** row appears in Profile settings showing your current address. Tap it, enter your new address, and confirm. You will need to re-enter your password for security. A verification link is then sent to the new address. Your sign-in email updates once you tap the link.

### Changing Your Password

If your account uses email/password sign-in, tap **Change password** in Profile settings. You will be prompted to confirm your identity by entering your current password, then enter a new one. A toast confirms success.

### Forgot Password

On the sign-in screen, tap **Log in**, enter your email address, then tap **Forgot password**. Instructions are sent to that address. If you do not see the email, check your spam folder.

### Linking an Email/Password Sign-In to an Apple Account

If you signed up through Apple and later want to add email/password login, a **Link email sign in** row appears in Profile settings. Tap it to enter an email and password that will work as an alternative sign-in method going forward.

### Logging Out

Tap **Logout** in Profile settings. You will be asked to confirm. After logging out, you return to the sign-in screen.

### Merging Two Accounts

If you accidentally created two separate Squabbit profiles, tap **Merge with another account** in Profile settings. The flow works as follows:

1. Search for the duplicate account you want to merge.
2. Sign in with the credentials for that other account so Squabbit can confirm you own both. The sign-in page shows the account's name with a **masked email** (for example "Sign in to Jane's account (j•••@gmail.com)") so you can confirm it's the right one, and lists that account's available sign-in methods. If you can't remember the password, tap **Forgot password?** to send a reset link to that account's email address.
3. Choose which sign-in method you want to keep going forward.
4. Confirm the merge. Data from both accounts is combined and the duplicate's login is deleted.

This action cannot be undone. If the merge fails partway through, the duplicate profile becomes an unauthenticated record. You can finish the process by opening that profile and using the merge option there.

### Deleting Your Account

In Profile settings, tap **Delete account** (shown in red). You will see the warning: "Are you sure you want to delete your account? This will also delete all your data and cannot be undone." Tap Delete to confirm. Squabbit requires a recent sign-in to complete deletion. If you see the message "Delete account failed — Deleting your account requires a recent sign in. Try logging out, logging back in and then delete account again," log out, log back in, and try the deletion again immediately.

Account deletion removes all of your data including rounds and scorecards. It cannot be reversed.

### Viewing the App Version

The current app version number is shown at the bottom of Profile settings (mobile only). This is useful when contacting support.

### Searching Group Settings

Tournament, league, and club settings pages can be long. To jump to a specific setting, use the **Search settings** box on the Settings tab (there's a **Search advanced settings** box for the advanced section). Type a keyword like "pace," "marker," "fees," or "handicap" and the list filters to just the matching tiles, hiding everything else. Clear the search to see the full settings list again.

### Help and Support

On the sign-in screen, tap the three-dot menu and choose **Help**. From your profile, tap the three-dot menu and choose **Help**. Both open the support triage page where you can browse common topics or send a message to the Squabbit team. The triage page also has an **Ask Squabbit AI** tile that opens the AI assistant (see below).

## The Squabbit AI Assistant

Squabbit has a built-in **AI assistant** you can chat with in plain language. It comes in two flavors:

- **General help chat**: a question-and-answer helper for how-tos ("How do I add a flight?"). Open it from the **Help** tab's **Ask Squabbit AI** tile, or from the **Search settings** box on a group's settings. It answers questions but doesn't change anything.
- **Group assistant**: available to **admins** on a tournament or league. It answers questions about that event AND can actually make setup changes for you.

### Opening the Group Assistant

On any tournament or league you administer, tap the **sparkle icon** (four-point star) at the top of the page. That opens the assistant with your event's context loaded, so it knows your rounds, formats, roster, and settings.

### How Actions Work

Describe what you want in your own words, for example "Set round 2 to the blue tees," "Make this a Ryder Cup with best ball day 1 and singles day 2 and pair everyone up," or "Add a $10 skins game and closest-to-the-pin on hole 7." The assistant replies with a **proposed changes** card listing each change it plans to make, with **Apply** and **Cancel** buttons. **Nothing changes until you tap Apply.** Every change is validated first; if something isn't allowed (for example deleting a round that already has scores) it's blocked and explained. Applying changes is free (it is not a Host Pro feature), and it's available to any admin.

### What the Tournament Assistant Can Do

It can set up and adjust almost anything you could configure by hand, including:

- **Rounds and schedule**: create or delete rounds, set a round's course tees, shift or set start times, shotgun/hole settings, lock rounds.
- **Formats and games**: add formats, set the scoring/game (net stroke, skins, stableford, etc.), configure multi-game formats like **Ryder Cup** (which game each day plays), tweak game and format settings, add cut rules.
- **Flights and teams**: create/edit/delete flights (with handicap, gender, or age bounds) and teams (including blind/padding members).
- **Pairings and tee times**: generate tee times, create a specific tee-time group, move or swap players between groups, set match pairings, and auto-generate matches or a bracket.
- **Money**: add/edit fees, set format purses and hole-competition purses (with payout distribution and opt-outs), add expenses, and record a player paid or paid out.
- **Roster**: edit a player's handicap/name/tee/gender/custom fields, set participation type, grant or revoke admin, mark DNF, set a per-round handicap override, add substitutes or ghosts.
- **Other**: add custom registration fields, adjust the registration form, post an announcement to chat, and mark the tournament complete.

It intentionally **cannot delete the tournament**.

### What the League Assistant Can Do

For a league it can create and schedule events (individually or on a recurring cadence), rename or reschedule events, add and configure league formats and points tables, set league handicap rules, manage fees, and post announcements. Its standout ability is **bulk per-event setup**: one request like "Add a $5 skins game to every event this season" applies the same tournament-level changes across many events at once.

### Handoffs and Limits

Some requests are better done on a real screen, so instead of acting the assistant offers a button that **opens that screen** (for example an "Add players" button). Like any AI it can be wrong, so a disclaimer sits above the input box; you can thumbs-up or thumbs-down any reply, and there's a **Contact support** link if it isn't helping.

## Host Pro and Purchases

### What Host Pro Is

**Host Pro** is Squabbit's paid organizer tier (previously called Squabbit Pro). It unlocks the professional hosting tools on a tournament, league, or club:

- **Printable scorecards and cart signs** (plus tee sheets, hole competition signs, and brackets)
- **Your own tournament website** (the public HTML website and leaderboard embed)
- **Sponsor logos and banners**
- **AI scorecard import** (Score from Image)
- **Auto-scrolling leaderboard** for clubhouse TVs

Everything else in Squabbit stays free: creating and running tournaments, leagues, and clubs, live scoring, leaderboards, handicaps, chat, and payments, with no player limits.

### Unlocking Host Pro

Pro features are marked with a gold **PRO** badge on their settings tiles. Tapping one without an active unlock opens the **Host Pro** screen ("Everything you need to run a polished, professional tournament") with the feature list and the purchase options:

- **Subscribe to Host Pro**: a yearly subscription that covers unlimited events, all year, across all of your groups.
- **Unlock just this event**: a one-time payment that unlocks a single tournament (tournaments only).

The unlock is shared per group: once one admin unlocks Host Pro for a group, every admin of that group can use the Pro features there. Prices and the available purchase paths vary by platform and region; on some platforms the purchase completes through a secure web checkout, and the feature unlocks once the payment goes through.

### Tournament Stats Unlock

In some tournaments, the detailed stats view (per-hole breakdowns, scoring trends, and the AI tournament recap) requires a one-time **Unlock stats** purchase. On mobile it's pay-what-you-can: pick any of the suggested amounts; every price unlocks the same stats. The unlock applies to that tournament for your account.

### The Purchases Screen

Open **Profile settings** and tap **Purchases** (in the **Money** section) to see everything you've bought: Host Pro subscriptions, per-event Host Pro unlocks, and tournament stats unlocks. Each purchase shows the product, the tournament it applies to (when relevant), the date, the amount, and its status (Active, Ended, Refunded, or "Active until {date}, won't renew" for a cancelled subscription).

- **Manage subscription** appears on an active subscription and opens where the subscription is managed: the App Store or Play Store for mobile purchases, or the Stripe billing portal for web purchases. Cancel, or update payment details, from there.
- **Restore purchases** re-checks your purchases with the store and the Squabbit server. Use it if something you bought shows as locked, for example after reinstalling the app or switching devices.

## Common Error Messages and What Causes Them

### "Authentication error" / "There was a problem logging in"

This dialog appears when something unexpected goes wrong during the sign-in process after your Firebase credentials are validated. It is not a wrong-password error. It means Squabbit's server could not load or create your player profile. Try again; if the problem persists, contact support. This is distinct from a network error (see below).

### "Can't reach Squabbit" / Network errors at sign-in

If your device cannot connect to Squabbit's servers during sign-in, you will see "Can't reach Squabbit" with the message: "Your account is fine — your device couldn't reach our server to load your profile." This is a transient network issue, not an account problem. The steps to resolve it are:

1. Toggle between Wi-Fi and cellular data.
2. Disable any VPN, and avoid corporate, school, or guest networks if possible.
3. Make sure your device date and time are set to automatic.
4. Fully close the app and reopen it.

If those steps do not help, contact support. Your account data is safe.

### "Incorrect email or password. Please try again."

The email/password combination you entered does not match any account. Double-check for typos. If you forgot your password, use the Forgot password flow. If you signed up with Google or Apple originally, you do not have a separate email/password login. Use the social sign-in button instead.

### "An account with this email already exists. Please log in instead."

You tried to create a new account with an email that is already registered. Tap the Log in button and use that email to sign in. If you forgot the password, use Forgot password.

### "No account found with this email. Please sign up first."

You tried to log in with an email that has no matching account. Tap Sign Up to create one, or check whether you used a different email address when you registered.

### "Too many attempts. Please wait a moment and try again."

Too many failed login attempts have temporarily locked that email address. Wait a few minutes and try again.

### "An account already exists with this email using a different sign-in method."

You tried to sign in through Google or Apple with an email that was originally registered with email/password (or vice versa). Use the original sign-in method, or tap "Link email sign in" in Profile settings once you are in.

### "Error signing in with Google. Please try again." / "Error signing in with Apple. Please try again."

A problem occurred with the Google or Apple authentication flow before reaching Squabbit's servers, usually a cancelled sign-in, a network interruption, or a device configuration issue. Try again. If Apple sign-in consistently fails, check your Apple ID settings on the device.

### WHS Handicap Lookup Errors

When a WHS/GHIN lookup fails, you may see one of these specific messages inside the error dialog:

- **"A profile with the provided ID was not found"**: The ID you entered does not match any record in that country's handicap system. Double-check the number; GHIN IDs in the US are typically 7 digits.
- **"The handicap for this profile has not been set"**: The profile exists but has no active handicap index. This happens for newly registered golfers whose first scores have not yet been processed by their national authority.
- **"There was a problem looking up the handicap. Please try again later."**: A temporary API error. Wait a few minutes and tap Refresh.
- **"The country is not supported"**: You selected a country for which automatic lookup is not available (for example Australia, the UK, or New Zealand). You can still enter your handicap manually.

### "Payment failed or was cancelled"

This toast appears after a Stripe checkout attempt that did not succeed. Either you cancelled the checkout flow or the card was declined. No charge was made. Try again with a different payment method, or contact your card issuer.

### "Could not open checkout page. Please try again."

The Stripe checkout page failed to launch. This can happen due to a network issue or a pop-up blocker on web. On the web, allow pop-ups for app.squabbitgolf.com and try again.

### "Account creation failed"

Squabbit could not create a new Stripe account for receiving registration payments. You can retry, or try the setup from a web browser at www.squabbitgolf.com.

### "Only players on this scorecard and admins can edit the hole scores."

You are attempting to edit scores on a scorecard you are not part of. If your name appears on the scorecard but you still see this message, you likely have two separate Squabbit accounts and the other profile was added to this event. Use the merge flow to combine your accounts, or ask the tournament admin to add the correct profile.

### "The tournament admin has enabled admin only mode where only admins can enter hole scores."

The organizer has restricted score entry to admins only. You cannot enter your own scores in this mode. Only an admin or an assigned marker can record them.

### "This round in the tournament has been locked by the admin and can no longer be edited."

The tournament admin has locked a completed round. Scores cannot be changed by players, though admins retain editing access.

### "Course not found" / "The course used for this round no longer exists."

The course linked to your scorecard is no longer in the database. The app will prompt you to select a replacement course so the scorecard can continue to function correctly.

### "Could not find a matching tee for '{name}' at {course}"

During a round or CSV import, the tee name stored in your data does not match any tee currently in the course record. This usually happens after a course is updated. Select the correct tee from the available options.

### "Failed to create tournament csv" / "Failed to create league csv" / "Failed to create scorecard csv"

The CSV export encountered a server or data error. Try again. If the error persists, contact support.

### "Deleting your account requires a recent sign in. Try logging out, logging back in and then delete account again."

Firebase requires a recent authentication session for account deletion. Log out, sign back in, and immediately attempt the deletion again.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Why isn't my handicap updating?

The behavior depends on your handicap source. If you are using **WHS**, your handicap is fetched automatically each time you open the app or are added to an event. You can also trigger an immediate refresh by going to Profile settings, tapping **Handicap index**, and tapping **Refresh**. If you see an error, check that your WHS ID and country are correct. If you are using the **Squabbit Index**, your handicap updates automatically after every round you mark as counting toward handicap. If you have no completed rounds, the index cannot be calculated yet. If you chose **Manual**, your handicap only changes when you edit it yourself.

### Why doesn't my net score show handicap strokes on par 3s?

Some tournament rounds are configured with **Gross scores on par 3** enabled. When that setting is active, only gross scores are used on par 3 holes. Handicap strokes do not apply to those holes regardless of your playing handicap. This is a round-level setting that the tournament admin controls. If you believe it should not be enabled, contact your tournament organizer.

### How do I delete a tournament?

Only the **tournament owner** (not just any admin) can delete a tournament. Open the tournament, tap the gear icon to go to settings, scroll to find the delete option, and confirm. Deletion removes the tournament and all associated rounds and scorecards permanently. If you are not the owner, you will not see the delete option. You can transfer ownership to yourself by asking the current owner to do so first.

### Can I undo a deletion?

Deleted scorecards, tournament rounds, and social rounds are moved to a **Recently Deleted** bin and kept for 30 days before being permanently removed. To recover an item, go to the tournament settings and look for the recovery option, or access it from the Groups page overflow menu. Tap the item you want and choose **Restore**. If two users have conflicting rounds for the same slot, you will be asked whether to replace the existing round or keep both. Full tournament and league deletions are also recoverable within the same 30-day window.
