### How do I leave a tournament, league, or club?

Open the group, tap the three-dot or gear menu, and choose **Leave**. For a tournament the prompt reads: "Are you sure you want to leave this tournament? You will need to re-enter the invite code if you want to see it again." For a league or club the prompt is similar. Note: the **owner** of a group cannot leave without either transferring ownership to another member first or deleting the group entirely.

### Can I change a tournament's course mid-event after scoring has begun?

The tournament course can be changed at any time from the tournament round settings. However, changing the tee selection after rounds have been played will automatically reassign existing unplayed rounds to the new tees, and you will see a confirmation message showing how many rounds were updated. Completed rounds that already have scores are not affected. Those scores were recorded against the original tees and course rating. Changes to tees can affect playing handicap calculations going forward.

### Where do my scores go after a tournament ends?

All rounds you play in Squabbit, whether social or tournament, are stored on your profile permanently. Open your profile and tap the **Rounds** tab to browse your history, filter by course, and view scorecards. Stats such as average score, handicap trend, and scoring averages are visible on the **Stats** tab. Tournament rounds continue to appear in your history alongside social rounds.

### How do players start entering scores when the round begins?

There is no separate "make the round live" switch, and an admin does not have to open the round for everyone. Each player simply opens their own scorecard and starts entering scores. The most direct way, and the one that works any time (including before the round day):

- Open the **Schedule** tab, tap the **My rounds** chip, and tap **Scorecard** on your round card.

Other ways to reach the same scorecard:

- On the **day of the round**, a green **Begin scoring Round N** banner also appears at the top of the leaderboard. Tap it. (It uses the tournament's timezone, so it shows on the correct day wherever your phone is set.)
- Tap the small **play icon** on your own row in the leaderboard.
- On the **Schedule** tab, **Tee times** chip, each tee-time card has a **Start round** button that anyone in that group (or an admin) can tap.

The one requirement is that you are **on a tee time**, so a scorecard exists for you. If you are not, you will see "Not added to a tee time." An admin adds players to tee times from the **Schedule** tab, **Tee times** chip. Once you are on a tee time you can open your scorecard and enter scores right away, even before the round's start time.

### How do I export results?

Squabbit exports to a CSV spreadsheet at every level:

- **Tournament**: open the tournament, tap the three-dot menu, and choose **Export as CSV**. This exports the players, every format's leaderboard, and all scorecards (hole by hole) for the whole event.
- **League**: from the league settings, choose **Export to CSV**. This is a single combined spreadsheet covering **all of the league's events at once**: every player alongside their results and standings across every tournament in the season. Use this one when you want a single file of all players, the events they played, and their finishing positions.
- **Scorecard**: open a scorecard, tap the menu, and choose **Export as CSV** for that one round.
- **Club**: from the club settings, choose **Export to CSV** for the member list.

If an export fails you will see a toast such as "Failed to create tournament csv." Try again or contact support.

### The course's slope, rating, par, or stroke index is wrong. How do I fix it?

Course data lives on the **course itself**, and you can edit it directly. Open the course (tap the course name on the scorecard, or find it in course search), tap the **three-dot menu**, and choose **Edit course**. In edit mode you can tap any **par**, **stroke index (S.I.)**, or **yardage** cell to change it, and edit a tee's **name, rating, and slope** from the tee selector's menu (**Edit tee**). You can add tees here too, for example a set of ladies' tees.

Because a course is **shared by everyone who plays it**, Squabbit warns you that your edits will be visible to all groups using that course before you save. Fixing the data once corrects it everywhere. If you would rather not change the shared record, create your own version of the course instead.

### How do I add a course that isn't in Squabbit, or a 9, 12, or 27-hole course?

From course search, tap **Create course**. Squabbit first asks **how many holes**: choose **9**, **18**, **27** (a multi-nine course), or **Other** to type any custom count such as **12**. Then pick how to enter the data:

- **Scan a scorecard**: take photos of the course's scorecard and Squabbit's AI reads the ratings, slopes, pars, stroke indexes, and yardages; you review and fix anything before saving.
- **Manual entry**: Squabbit walks you field by field (name, location, tee, rating, slope, then per-hole pars, S.I., and yardages).

Multi-nine courses (27, 36, or more) store a name, rating, and slope for **each nine** independently. A 9-hole course can be played as 9, looped to 18, or combined with another nine.

### What does "Promote to official course" do?

A course you create is a **user-created** course, which only shows in search when "Include user created courses" is on. **Promote to official course** (in the course's three-dot menu) sends a request to make it a regular, always-visible Squabbit course. Before promoting, make sure every tee has a name, rating, and slope, every hole has a par, stroke index, and yardage, and the name and location are correct. If an official course with the same name already exists nearby, Squabbit tells you instead of creating a duplicate.

### How do I change the course or tees for my event?

An admin can change or add a course from the round itself: on the scorecard (or the tee-time card), tap the **course name** at the top, then pick another course in the round or **Search for a course** to add one. When you add a course you choose which **tees** are available for that round. You can do this even after players have registered; existing scores stay on their scorecards. If you cannot find the course you want in search, type its name to reveal the **User created courses** tab (user-made courses only appear once you search), or create it with **Create course**.

### How do I add players to my league, not just to one event?

There are two separate places, and confusing them is a common snag:

- **The league roster** (members for the whole season): open the **league**, go to the **Players** tab, and use the **add** button. This is where you add someone to the base league so they are available in every event.
- **A single event** (one tournament in the league): when you add players inside an event you pick from your existing **league members**, or use **Add guest** (a one-off player for just that event) or **Add substitute** (a fill-in for an absent member). If the only choices you see are **Add guest** and **Add substitute**, you are on the event, add the person to the league **Players** tab first and they will then appear in every event.

Removed a member by mistake? Add them again from the league **Players** tab; their past results stay attached to their profile.

### How do I copy an event from one league or season into another?

For a **whole new season** of the same league, use **Create new season** from the league card menu and tick **Copy events**. Squabbit builds fresh copies of every event, shifted forward by a year, in a new independent league (the old season is left untouched).

For a **single event** into another league, there is one extra step. **Import tournaments** only lists tournaments that are not already in a league, so an event already sitting in your old league will not show up there. First open that event, go to tournament settings, and tap **Copy tournament**, the copy comes out standalone (not in any league). Then open the destination league, tap **Import tournaments**, and pick the copy.

If you do not need to keep the event in the old league, you can instead **move** it straight over from the event's card menu (move to another league).

For sharing live scores publicly, tournaments have a **Public page** option in settings. Tap it to copy a link that anyone can open in a browser without signing up. You can also enable a public website for your event from the Website section of tournament settings, which creates a landing page with live leaderboard access.

### How do I add a player who isn't in Squabbit yet?

When adding players to an event, tap the green + button and choose **Create user**. Enter the player's first name, last name, and optionally their email address and WHS ID. If you provide an email address, Squabbit sends an automatic invite. Once the player signs up using that email, their account links automatically to the profile you created. All their existing rounds and group memberships carry over. You do not need to do anything extra after they sign up.

Alternatively, you can import multiple players at once using a CSV file. The CSV needs at minimum a First and Last column. Optional columns include Email, Gender, WHS ID, and Handicap. Download a template from the import screen for the correct format.

### How do I import past rounds, or set up handicaps from previous scores?

You can load historical rounds so they feed handicaps, which is handy for a new league where players already have a scoring history:

- **One player's history**: open that player's **profile**, go to the **Rounds** tab, and tap **Import rounds** (also available in the profile's overflow menu), then upload their CSV. A league admin can also import a player's rounds from the **league settings** using the **Import player rounds** tile (pick the player). You can import **total scores only** (no hole-by-hole needed), and **9-hole rounds are supported**. Squabbit works out each round's differential and, if the player uses the **Squabbit Index**, folds them into their index right away.
- **A whole past event into a league**: a league admin uses **Import tournaments** in the **league settings** to upload a CSV of a tournament that was played outside Squabbit. It becomes an event linked to the league so those results count in the season standings. Each row needs the player's name, round, course, tee, and a Total or Differential.

Download the blank template from the import screen for the exact columns. Imported rounds feed the **Squabbit Index** only; they do not change an official **WHS/GHIN** index, which comes from your national authority.

### Can someone view a tournament without a Squabbit account?

Yes. Every tournament can generate a **public page** link (found in tournament settings) that anyone can open in a browser to see the event details and live leaderboard. No account is needed. If the organizer has also enabled a **website**, there is a full landing page available.

Anyone can also scan the tournament's **QR code** or open its **invite link** without an account. They're asked whether they're playing or just spectating. Spectators follow the leaderboard live; people who choose "I'm playing" enter their name, get matched to their spot on the leaderboard, and can enter their own scores as a guest. (Scanning a printed scorecard or cart-sign QR drops them straight onto that scorecard.) No one else needs to sign up for a player to keep score. If they later create a Squabbit account, the guest session converts to a full account.

### What languages does Squabbit support?

Squabbit is available in 11 languages: English, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian Bokmål, Dutch, Portuguese, and Swedish. The app uses your device language by default. To override it, go to Profile settings, tap **Language**, and choose from the list. The change applies immediately.

### Can I use Squabbit on the web?

Yes. The full Squabbit app is available at **app.squabbitgolf.com**. It has the same features as the mobile app and is useful for entering players with a keyboard, uploading CSV files, or displaying a live leaderboard on a clubhouse TV. From within any tournament or league you can also find a **View in a browser** option in settings that opens that specific group in the web app.

### Why does my profile show "WHS" below my handicap but the number isn't current?

Your WHS handicap is refreshed automatically when you open the app. If you recently posted scores to your national handicap authority and the new index is not reflected yet, tap **Refresh** on the Handicap index page in Profile settings to trigger an immediate lookup. If the lookup fails, check that your WHS ID and country selection are correct. Keep in mind that national authorities like GHIN typically update handicap indexes on a regular schedule. If your new scores were only posted recently, the official index may not have been updated yet on their end.

### As an admin, how do I change a player's handicap for my event?

Two different numbers are involved, and mixing them up is the usual source of confusion:

- The **tournament handicap** is the player's handicap **for this event**. An admin can **always** change it: open the event's **Players** list, tap the player, tap their **handicap**, and enter a new value in the **Edit handicap** dialog. It is captured from the player's index when they join and stays fixed for the event unless you change or refresh it. For a multi-round tournament you can also set a **per-round handicap override** that applies to a single round.
- The player's **handicap index** lives on **their own profile**. An admin can only edit that for a player **they created who has not registered yet**. Once a player registers and manages their own account, only **they** can change their index (from Profile settings). If you need a different number for your event, change the **tournament handicap** instead, which always works.

If the player's handicap is linked to **WHS/GHIN**, tapping their handicap also offers a **Refresh** option that re-pulls their official index. If tapping seems to do nothing, make sure you are an **admin** and are opening the player from the **Players** list, not the read-only leaderboard.

### Why can I see my name on a scorecard but can't edit the scores?

The most common cause is that you have two Squabbit accounts and the other profile was the one added to the event. You can verify this by checking whether the name shown on the scorecard matches the account you are currently signed in to. To fix it, use **Merge with another account** in Profile settings to combine the two profiles into one, or ask the tournament admin to remove the wrong profile and add your current one.

### How do I find my WHS or GHIN ID?

Your WHS or GHIN ID is the account number assigned by your national golf authority. In North America it is your GHIN number, typically 7 digits, found on the GHIN app or your club's management system. In other supported countries check with your club or national federation. Enter this number in Profile settings under Handicap index after selecting WHS as your source.

### What happens if my country's WHS lookup isn't supported?

If you selected a country that does not have a publicly accessible API (such as the UK, Australia, Ireland, or New Zealand), you will see a message explaining that automatic lookup is not available. You can still play and track your handicap by choosing **Manual** as your source and entering your current index. Squabbit also has a **Squabbit Index** option that calculates a handicap from rounds you record in the app.

### How do I share my profile with another golfer?

Tap the QR code icon in the top-right corner of your profile page. A dialog shows your name and a QR code. Other golfers can scan it to open your profile directly. You can also tap **Share link** in that dialog to send your profile link through any messaging app.

### Why can't I follow a player who appears in search?

Following is approval-based, so tapping **Follow** sends the player a request rather than following them instantly. The button then shows **Requested** until they approve it; once approved you'll see their rounds, stats, and activity. If the button already shows **Following**, you're approved and following them. You can tap **Requested** to cancel a pending request.

### Can I block another user?

Yes. From any user's profile, the three-dot menu includes a block option. Blocked users are managed through your settings. Blocking prevents that user from seeing your profile and activity.

### My tournament registration failed because of a league membership check. What does that mean?

Some leagues require that only existing league members can register for events within that league. If you receive a message like "The event admin has specified that only league members can register for this event," it means the organizer has restricted registration to people already in the league roster. If you believe you should have access (for example because you are in the league under a different profile), ask the league admin to add your current profile, or use the account merge flow if you have duplicate profiles.

### I'm playing a social/practice round — how do I stop it from counting toward my handicap?

Open the scorecard, tap your **name or avatar** on your row, and turn off **Count towards handicap**. The round still saves with your scores and stats but is excluded from your Squabbit-calculated Handicap Index. You can flip it back on any time. This setting has no effect if your handicap source is GHIN or WHS — that index comes from the external system.

### How do I give a team a head start on the leaderboard (e.g. -6 strokes before play begins)?

Open the tournament leaderboard, tap the team's name (admin only), and choose **Modify team score**. Enter a negative number (e.g. -6) to subtract strokes, or a positive number to add. The leaderboard updates immediately and the adjustment carries through as holes are scored. This is the only way to make a team visibly start at -6. Team handicaps distribute strokes during play but never show a starting adjustment on the leaderboard.

The same option exists for individual players in non-team formats: tap the player's name on the leaderboard and choose **Modify user score**.

### How do I adjust a single player's score manually (bonus or penalty points)?

On the tournament leaderboard, tap the player's name (admin only) and choose **Modify user score**. Enter the adjustment value. This is applied to that player's leaderboard total in this format only. The same approach works on league leaderboards for season-wide adjustments.

### How do I switch the scorecard between yards and meters?

The unit of measurement is set per user, not per tournament. Open any scorecard, tap the overflow menu (three dots), tap the gear icon to open **Scorecard settings**, and use the **Unit of measurement** toggle. The choice is saved on your account and applies to distances on the scorecard, the map shot-tracker, and shot-distance dialogs everywhere in the app.

You can also change yards/meters from your profile settings under **Distance preference**.

### How do I hide players' contact info from the public roster?

Each group has two privacy controls. Open the group settings and find the **Players visible** (or **Members visible** for a club) switch — turn it off to hide the player list entirely from non-admins. To keep the list visible but hide individual columns like Email or Phone, open the players list, tap the overflow menu, and use the visibility dialog to uncheck the columns you want kept private. Name is always shown; everything else (Handicap, WHS ID, Email, Phone, DOB, Gender, Transport, Tee, Fee, Total fees, Paid, Won, Paid out, Owes) can be toggled.

### Why aren't a player's scores showing on the leaderboard?

Several things can keep a player (or their scores) off a leaderboard. Since it depends on the specific setup, check these in order, most common first:

- **You're viewing the wrong format or round.** Leaderboards are per-format and per-round. Tap the correct **format chip** at the top (a player entered only in Net won't show on the Gross board, and vice-versa), and check the **round** tab if the tournament has more than one round.
- **The player isn't a participant in that format.** A player can be on the tournament but not included in every format. In the tournament settings, open the format and confirm the player is in its participant list.
- **The scores were entered in a casual round, not the tournament round.** Scores only count when the scorecard is linked to the tournament's round. If someone started a standalone "play a round" scorecard, those scores won't appear on the tournament leaderboard, enter them on the scorecard opened from the tournament round instead.
- **A flight filter is hiding them.** If the format uses flights, the leaderboard may be filtered to one flight. Switch the flight chip (or check "Unassigned") to see players in other flights.
- **The player is marked DNF** (did not finish) for that format, which removes them from its rankings. Open the player in the tournament and clear the DNF toggle if it shouldn't be set.
- **The player missed the cut** in a format that has cut rules. This is expected, cut players appear below the cut line, so scroll down.
- **In a team format, individuals sit under their team.** Expand the team row to see each member; they don't get their own top-level row.
- **League only: the event isn't counting yet.** If the league has **Only count completed events** turned on, an event's scores don't count until that event is marked **complete**. Also, if the league sets a **minimum number of events to rank**, a player below that threshold won't appear ranked until they've played enough events.

If none of these fit, tap **Contact us** with the tournament/league name and the player, and we can look at the setup.

### Why does my league's leaderboard look stale after I edited an old event?

Squabbit recomputes leaderboards in real time as scores come in, but if you edit an old event's scores after the fact, the league's cumulative standings and league-wide stats can fall behind. From the league's settings, scroll to the **Other** section and tap **Recalculate leaderboards and stats**. Confirm the (expensive) operation and the league rebuilds everything from the underlying tournament rounds.

### Can I set a different handicap for simulator rounds?

Yes. If a simulator handicap has been configured for your account (typically set by a league admin who runs simulator events), it appears as a separate **Simulator handicap index** row on your Profile settings page. Rounds played on a simulator are tracked separately and contribute to a standalone simulator handicap index rather than your on-course index. You can tap the row to see how it was calculated.

### How do I pay my entry fee, or settle up what I owe?

Open the group's **Money** tab (or tap **View Registration**) to see your balance. If you owe money, tap **Pay** and choose how: **Credit card** (processed through Stripe, clears instantly) or a peer-to-peer app like **Venmo** or **Zelle** (you'll see the recipient's handle/QR, send the money in that app, then tap **I've paid**). For peer-to-peer payments the recipient confirms they received it, then your balance clears. A pending pill shows while a payment is awaiting confirmation. See the Payments section for the full picture.

### Someone owes me money in a group: how do I collect?

Open your balance and find the row for who owes you. As the person being paid you can **Request payment** (sends them a reminder notification) or, if they've already paid you outside the app, **Mark as paid** to clear the balance yourself. Make sure you've saved a payment method on your profile (**Profile settings → Payment methods**) so people know how to pay you.

### How do we split shared costs like food or lodging?

Use **shared expenses** on the group's **Money** tab. Tap **+ Add expense**, enter the amount and category (Food, Lodging, Transport, Course fees, Other), pick who paid, and choose how to split it (equally or custom amounts per person). Each person's share shows up on their balance, and the group settles it all up alongside fees and winnings. Admins can let players add their own expenses by turning on **Add expenses** in the money settings.

### How do I mark a game or purse as paid?

It depends on how the purse was set up, and there are two different places to do it:

- **A purse on a tournament format, or a hole competition** (set up by the admin in the tournament settings) is held by the group treasury and settles through the **Money** tab → **Balances**. Tap the winning player to open their receipt, then use **Mark as paid** / **+ Add payment** to record the payout.
- **A side game added to a scorecard** (e.g. a scorecard skins or nassau bet) settles **directly between the players on that scorecard**, not through the Money tab. Open the scorecard's payouts view, switch to the **Summary** tab, and use the **Pay / Request / Mark as paid** buttons on the settlement row.

If you're not sure which one you have, check where you set the purse up: on a tournament format → Money tab → Balances; added to a scorecard → the scorecard's Payouts → Summary.

### How do we settle a skins or matchplay side bet?

Side-game pots settle directly between players from the **Summary** tab of the scorecard's payouts view, not through the group balances. Each settlement row has **Pay**, **Request**, and **Mark as paid** buttons just like the group Balances page. This works for both tournament and casual social rounds.
